name: Hague Convention 2019 on the Recognition and Enforcement of Foreign Judgments (Hague Judgments Convention)
summary: The Hague Convention 2019 on the Recognition and Enforcement of Foreign Judgments (popularly the Hague Judgments Convention / formally Convention of 2 July 2019 on the Recognition and Enforcement of Foreign Judgments in Civil or Commercial Matters) is a global supranational meta-framework foreign-judgments-recognition + enforcement binding-treaty instrument adopted by the Hague Conference on Private International Law (HCCH) — structurally distinctive globally as the most-recent modern global binding-treaty framework operating specifically on cross-border foreign-judgments-recognition + enforcement framework (Hague Judgments Convention adopted 2 July 2019 in The Hague + effective 1 September 2023 — formal global binding-treaty framework establishing comprehensive recognition + enforcement framework for foreign civil + commercial judgments, distinct from Hague Convention 1980 + 1993 + 1996 + 2007 children's-frameworks which operate on family-law-specific frameworks, structurally distinctive globally as the most-recent modern global binding-treaty framework operating on formal-foreign-judgments-recognition-and-enforcement-civil-commercial-specific framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal post-Hague-2005 Choice of Court Convention judgments-recognition + enforcement elaboration framework (Hague Judgments Convention adopted as global comprehensive supplement to Hague Convention 2005 on Choice of Court Agreements framework + Hague Convention 2007 + Hague Convention 1996 frameworks — applicable to 6 state parties as of 2026 including European Union bloc + Ukraine + Uruguay + Israel + Cabo Verde + Costa Rica, structurally distinctive globally as the newest modern global binding-treaty framework with substantial state-party-coverage growth potential), as the only modern global binding-treaty framework operating formal exclusion of family-law + maintenance + insolvency frameworks (Convention Article 2 framework providing exclusion of family-law-related matters + maintenance obligations + insolvency-related matters from Convention applicability — formal family-law exclusion framework, distinct from Hague Convention 2007 + 1996 + 1993 + 1980 family-law-specific frameworks, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global binding-treaty framework with formal family-law exclusion framework), and as the central jurisdiction of the formal EU bloc 2022 accession framework (European Union bloc accession to Hague Judgments Convention 29 August 2022 + effective 1 September 2023 alongside Convention entry into force — formal EU bloc-level accession framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global binding-treaty framework with formal EU bloc-level accession framework). The Hague Judgments Convention is binding upon 6 state parties as of 2026 + 1 EU bloc state party framework — early-adoption framework with substantial growth potential. The Convention is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label, though formal Article 2 family-law exclusion framework explicitly excludes family-law matters from Convention applicability — operating as complementary framework with Hague Convention 1980 + 1993 + 1996 + 2007 frameworks. The Permanent Bureau of the Hague Conference on Private International Law is the apex monitoring framework body; national-level apex courts operate as judicial bodies. The Hague Judgments Convention is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label.

statutory_framework.citation: Hague Convention 2019 on the Recognition and Enforcement of Foreign Judgments
statutory_framework.title: Hague Judgments Convention
statutory_framework.relevance: Hague Convention on the Recognition and Enforcement of Foreign Judgments in Civil or Commercial Matters adopted 2 July 2019 in The Hague + effective 1 September 2023 — formal global binding-treaty framework establishing comprehensive recognition + enforcement framework for foreign civil + commercial judgments.

statutory_framework.citation: Hague Judgments Convention Article 2 (Family-Law Exclusion Framework)
statutory_framework.title: Hague Judgments Convention Article 2
statutory_framework.relevance: Hague Judgments Convention Article 2 framework providing exclusion of family-law-related matters + maintenance obligations + insolvency-related matters from Convention applicability — formal family-law exclusion framework.

statutory_framework.citation: Hague Convention 2005 on Choice of Court Agreements (predecessor framework)
statutory_framework.title: Hague Convention 2005
statutory_framework.relevance: Hague Convention 2005 on Choice of Court Agreements adopted 30 June 2005 + effective 1 October 2015 — predecessor framework to Hague Judgments Convention 2019.

statutory_framework.citation: EU Brussels Ia Regulation 1215/2012 (functional equivalent framework)
statutory_framework.title: EU Brussels Ia Regulation
statutory_framework.relevance: EU Council Regulation 1215/2012 of 12 December 2012 + effective 10 January 2015 — EU bloc functional equivalent framework operating cross-border-judgments-recognition + enforcement framework within EU bloc.

statutory_framework.citation: EU Bloc Accession to Hague Judgments Convention 2022
statutory_framework.title: EU Bloc Accession
statutory_framework.relevance: European Union bloc accession to Hague Judgments Convention 29 August 2022 — formal EU bloc-level accession framework.

apex_courts.name: Permanent Bureau of the Hague Conference on Private International Law (HCCH)
apex_courts.role: Apex monitoring framework body for Hague Judgments Convention.

apex_courts.name: Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU)
apex_courts.role: Court of Justice of the European Union — formal EU bloc judicial body for EU bloc accession framework.

anonymisation_convention: Hague Judgments Convention-related individual communications are anonymised per national-level court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Hague Convention 2005 on Choice of Court Agreements adopted
key_developments.description: Hague Convention 2005 on Choice of Court Agreements adopted 30 June 2005 — predecessor framework to Hague Judgments Convention 2019.

key_developments.title: Hague Convention 2005 enters force
key_developments.description: Hague Convention 2005 on Choice of Court Agreements entered force 1 October 2015 — formal global binding-treaty framework operational.

key_developments.title: Hague Judgments Convention adopted
key_developments.description: Hague Convention on the Recognition and Enforcement of Foreign Judgments in Civil or Commercial Matters adopted 2 July 2019 in The Hague — formal global binding-treaty framework.

key_developments.title: EU Bloc accession
key_developments.description: European Union bloc accession to Hague Judgments Convention 29 August 2022 — formal EU bloc-level accession framework.

key_developments.title: Hague Judgments Convention enters force
key_developments.description: Hague Convention on the Recognition and Enforcement of Foreign Judgments in Civil or Commercial Matters entered force 1 September 2023 — formal global binding-treaty framework operational.

structural_findings: Hague Judgments Convention operates a supranational global binding-treaty framework — places Hague Judgments Convention in the global supranational-meta-framework cluster.

Most-recent modern global binding-treaty framework operating specifically on cross-border foreign-judgments-recognition + enforcement framework is structurally distinctive globally — formal-foreign-judgments-recognition-and-enforcement-civil-commercial-specific framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal post-Hague-2005 Choice of Court Convention judgments-recognition + enforcement elaboration framework is structurally distinctive globally — newest modern global binding-treaty framework with substantial state-party-coverage growth potential.

Only modern global binding-treaty framework operating formal exclusion of family-law + maintenance + insolvency frameworks is structurally distinctive globally — formal family-law exclusion framework via Article 2.
Central jurisdiction of formal EU bloc 2022 accession framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global binding-treaty framework with formal EU bloc-level accession framework.
6 state party + 1 EU bloc state party ratification framework is structurally distinctive globally — early-adoption framework with substantial growth potential.

2005 Hague Convention + 2015 entry into force + 2019 Hague Judgments Convention + 2022 EU Bloc accession + 2023 entry into force cross-border-judgments-recognition-framework-development sequence is structurally distinctive globally.
Article 2 family-law exclusion framework operating as complementary framework with Hague Convention 1980 + 1993 + 1996 + 2007 family-law-specific frameworks is structurally distinctive globally.

sources.title: Hague Conference on Private International Law (HCCH)

editorial_notes: Hague Judgments Convention 2019 jurisdiction sidecar — supranational global cross-border foreign-judgments-recognition + enforcement binding treaty meta-framework (Hague Judgments Convention 2019/2023 + Article 2 Family-Law Exclusion Framework + Hague Convention 2005 on Choice of Court Agreements predecessor framework + EU Brussels Ia Regulation 1215/2012 functional equivalent + EU Bloc accession 2022). Most-recent modern global binding-treaty framework operating specifically on cross-border foreign-judgments-recognition + enforcement framework globally + central jurisdiction of formal post-Hague-2005 Choice of Court Convention judgments-recognition + enforcement elaboration framework + only modern global binding-treaty framework operating formal exclusion of family-law + maintenance + insolvency frameworks + central jurisdiction of formal EU bloc 2022 accession framework + 6 state party + 1 EU bloc state party ratification framework + 2005 Hague Convention + 2015 entry into force + 2019 Hague Judgments Convention + 2022 EU Bloc accession + 2023 entry into force cross-border-judgments-recognition-framework-development sequence + Article 2 family-law exclusion framework operating as complementary framework with Hague Convention 1980 + 1993 + 1996 + 2007 family-law-specific frameworks.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator. NOTE: Article 2 family-law exclusion framework explicitly excludes family-law matters from Convention applicability — operating as complementary framework with Hague Convention 1980 + 1993 + 1996 + 2007 frameworks. Hague Conference framework operates as complementary children's-frameworks quintet (1980 + 1993 + 1996 + 2007 + 2019-exclusion).

Joins global + supranational + global-foreign-judgments-recognition-enforcement-binding-treaty cluster + Hague-Judgments-Convention-2019-most-recent-globally-distinctive + Article-2-family-law-exclusion-framework + Hague-Convention-2005-Choice-of-Court-predecessor + EU-bloc-accession-2022 + EU-Brussels-Ia-Regulation-functional-equivalent + 6-state-party-1-EU-bloc-early-adoption-framework + complementary-to-Hague-1980-1993-1996-2007-children-family-conventions clusters within the corpus.
